doctrine: A page must carry what the reader needs. Do not score the page; number what it must prove it carries — so a how-to, a learn page, or a 'build your own AInternet' guide cannot silently miss a prerequisite, a guard, or strand the reader.

dimensions.no_banned_doctrine: Carries no legacy scalar trust-score language (except explicit deprecation/negation). The thing that would have caught the 2026-06-28 residue.
dimensions.identity_first: Establishes WHO acts (identity / JIS / .aint) before, or alongside, the mechanics — never IP-first.
dimensions.prerequisites: States what the reader needs up front: install line, prior page, or what must be running.
dimensions.runnable: Has at least one concrete, copy-pasteable code or CLI block.
dimensions.guard: Names the relevant default where an action carries risk: fail-closed, dark-by-default, consent (SNAFT), isolation (Airlock), or posture.
dimensions.next_step: Links the reader onward (a Related/Next section or outbound page links). Never a dead end.
dimensions.machine_surface: Has or links a machine-actionable counterpart (resources.json / api.json / a curl path) — because an AI curls, it does not read.
dimensions.conformance_pointer: Points at conformance vectors. Docs explain; vectors decide.

banned_doctrine.replacement_hint: Use route posture / proven route / required_posture / posture transition. See learn/route-posture.md.
